title: Process for reducing pattern-induced wafer deformation
abstract: A semiconductor device wafer includes a plurality of device patterns formed in or over a semiconductor substrate, and a scribe area from which the device patterns are excluded. A plurality of dummy features are located in at least one material level in the scribe area, including over laser scribe dots formed in the semiconductor substrate.
claims: 1. A semiconductor device wafer, comprising: a plurality of device patterns formed in or over a semiconductor substrate having an edge at an outer perimeter of the substrate, the device patterns being arranged in an array of rows and columns; a scribe area from which said device patterns are excluded, the scribe area being located between a topmost row of the device patterns and the edge of the semiconductor substrate, or between a bottommost row of the device patterns and the edge of the semiconductor substrate; and a plurality of dummy features formed in at least one material level over the substrate in said scribe area, a longest dimension of the dummy features being shorter than a width of the device patterns.
